title: Week 2 Day 1 (2/2) — LiteLLM, caching, multi-bot
summary: Part 2 of 2 · LiteLLM costs · prompt caching · LangChain peek · two-model loop.

sections.heading: LiteLLM
points: Use when you want one interface and billing visibility across vendors.
sections.code:
from litellm import completion
response = completion(model="openai/gpt-4.1", messages=question)

sections.heading: Prompt caching
sections.remember: Put **long static** content first (policy, Hamlet, docs) · **variable** user question last — repeat calls cache the prefix → cheaper.
points: Enterprise pattern: big system doc + small user delta.

sections.heading: LangChain (light touch)
sections.remember: `ChatOpenAI(model=...).invoke(messages)` — heavy framework; course shows it briefly; **raw API is fine** for most work.
points: Know it exists; don't assume you must use it in production.

sections.heading: Multi-bot loop
sections.remember: Two models alternate — rebuild full **`messages`** each turn with different **system** prompts + entire history.
points: Debate, simulation, role-play — cost grows with every turn (full context resent).
Pitfall: optional keys missing → skip that provider.

cheatSheet: Cache: long static first, user last
Multi-bot = full messages history each call
